topics: What is Yoga and its history
The eight steps of Ashtanga Yoga
Yama - five rules for living with others
Aparigraha - the power of being content
subject: Physical Education

sections.heading: 🧙 The Ancient Quest Called Yoga
sections.body: Imagine a wizard who discovered a magical spell thousands of years ago in India — a spell that makes your body strong, your mind calm and your heart happy. That spell is called Yoga! The word Yoga comes from a Sanskrit word 'yuj' which means to join or connect — like how a cricket team joins together to win a match. Yoga connects your body, mind and inner self so everything works in harmony.
keyPoints: Yoga is NOT just stretching — it is a science of healthy and happy living
Yoga started in ancient India and today it is practised in more than 190 countries
International Day of Yoga is celebrated on 21st June every year

sections.heading: ⚔️ The Eight Powers of Ashtanga Yoga
sections.body: A great wizard named Maharshi Patanjali wrote down 196 rules of Yoga called Yoga Sutras — think of them as the ultimate quest guide! He described eight steps to reach the highest level of peace and happiness, called Samadhi. Just like in cricket you go from learning to hold the bat, then bowling, then fielding — each step builds on the last. These eight steps are your realm-unlocking powers!
keyPoints: The 8 steps are: Yama, Niyama, Asana, Pranayama, Pratyahara, Dharana, Dhyana and Samadhi
Asana means doing postures to make your body strong and flexible — like warming up before playing kabaddi
Samadhi is the final goal — a state of total peace and happiness, like winning the ultimate quest

sections.heading: 🛡️ Yama — The Five Spells of Living with Others
sections.body: Yama is the first power in your Yoga quest. These are five simple rules that help you live peacefully with people around you — your family, friends, classmates and even animals. Think of Yama as five magical spells that protect you and everyone around you. For example, Ahimsa means never hurting anyone — just like how you would never hurt your pet dog during play.
keyPoints: Ahimsa = be kind to all living things; Satya = always speak the truth like telling your parent if you broke something
Asteya = never steal; Brahmacharya = use your energy wisely — finish your homework before playing games
Aparigraha = do not be greedy — be happy with what you already have

sections.heading: 🌟 Aparigraha — The Mastery of Being Content
sections.body: Aparigraha is one of the most powerful spells in the Yoga quest — it means not wanting more and more things and being thankful for what you already have. Think about Diwali time when you want every single new toy in the shop — Aparigraha teaches you to stop and say, 'I already have enough!' India's great leader Lal Bahadur Shastri showed this mastery when he refused free sarees even as the Prime Minister, because he only wanted to spend what he could afford from his own money.
keyPoints: Aparigraha helps you live simply and stay happy — like enjoying your old cricket bat instead of always wanting a new one
Being grateful for what you have is a true sign of a Yoga hero
Shastri ji is a real-life example — he refused gifts because he believed in honest and simple living

sections.heading: 🙏 Starting and Ending Your Yoga Quest
sections.body: Every great quest begins and ends with respect. In Yoga, we start with an opening prayer where the student and teacher both ask for protection, wisdom and energy together — like a team huddle before a big match! We end with a closing prayer wishing happiness and good health for everyone in the world. Sit comfortably in Sukhasana, keep your back straight, close your eyes and breathe deeply — your Yoga quest begins right here!
keyPoints: The opening prayer asks that both teacher and student grow strong and wise together
The closing prayer wishes that all people everywhere be happy, healthy and free from suffering
Starting each session with calm breathing and 'Om' chanting helps your mind become focused and ready

summary: In this epic quest called Yoga, you discovered that Yoga is an ancient Indian science — not just exercise — that unites your body, mind and soul to make you strong, calm and happy. The great wizard Maharshi Patanjali gave us the eight-step Ashtanga Yoga path, starting with Yama — five golden rules for living kindly and honestly with others. You learned powerful spells like Ahimsa (kindness), Satya (truth) and the mighty Aparigraha (being content), inspired by the simple life of Lal Bahadur Shastri. Every Yoga session begins and ends with a prayer of peace — because a true Yoga hero cares not just for themselves but for the whole world. Begin your quest today, even five minutes of Yoga each day can unlock your greatest powers!
